items.title: `Error: Debug Failure. False expression` for mypy/config_parser.py:107:46

labels.name: addressed in next version
labels.description: Issue is fixed and will appear in next published version

items.body:
**Describe the bug**
In a fork of pyright (to make use of internal APIs), I use `Program.indexWorkspace` method to index an entire workspace in a batch-esque style https://github.com/Strum355/pyright/blob/a1c9990dbdbdd10aae482fcbc26e124a558fd032/packages/lsif-pyright/src/indexer.ts#L27. This causes the exception in the title to be thrown. Stacktrace can be found below.

While I understand pyright does not have a public API yet for a reason, we are evaluating pyright as a backend for semantic indexing of Python projects, and there may be a few roadbumps along the way we hope to get some assistance with :slightly_smiling_face: 

**To Reproduce**
1. Clone mypy repo
2. Clone fork of pyright
3. Point hard-coded path to clone of mypy
4. In packages/lsif-pyright, invoke `npm run webpack && node dist/lsif-pyright.js`

**Expected behavior**
using `Program.indexWorkspace` would work just as well, if not better, than via the language server.

**Screenshots or Code**
https://github.com/python/mypy/blob/master/mypy/config_parser.py#L107

```python
ini_config_types = {
    'python_version': parse_version,
    'strict_optional_whitelist': lambda s: s.split(),
	#          Flow type inference failed at ^^^^^
    'custom_typing_module': str,
    'custom_typeshed_dir': expand_path,
    'mypy_path': lambda s: [expand_path(p.strip()) for p in re.split('[,:]', s)],
    'files': split_and_match_files,
    'quickstart_file': expand_path,
    'junit_xml': expand_path,
    # SNIP
	...
}  # type: Final[Dict[str, _INI_PARSER_CALLABLE]]
```

**VS Code extension or command-line**
Custom CLI at https://github.com/Strum355/pyright/. Unfortunately the exception is not thrown with 1.1.144 of the vscode pyright extension with the same mypy file.

**Additional context**

<details>
<summary>Output from printing expression node </summary>

Console.log invoked [here](https://github.com/Strum355/pyright/blob/main/packages/pyright-internal/src/analyzer/typeEvaluator.ts#L16321)

```js
<ref *2> {
  start: 3594,
  length: 5,
  nodeType: 38,
  id: 16597,
  token: {
    start: 3594,
    length: 5,
    type: 7,
    value: 'split',
    comments: undefined
  },
  value: 'split',
  parent: <ref *1> {
    start: 3592,
    length: 7,
    nodeType: 35,
    id: 16598,
    leftExpression: {
      start: 3592,
      length: 1,
      nodeType: 38,
      id: 16596,
      token: [Object],
      value: 's',
      parent: [Circular *1]
    },
    memberName: [Circular *2],
    parent: {
      start: 3592,
      length: 9,
      nodeType: 9,
      id: 16599,
      leftExpression: [Circular *1],
      arguments: [],
      parent: [Object]
    }
  }
}
```

</details>

items.title: Pyright does not show error when mixin's attribute was not initialized

labels.name: as designed
labels.description: Not a bug, working as intended

items.body:
**Describe the bug**
Pyright does not show error when mixin's attribute was not initialized

**Expected behavior**
Pyright should show that initialization of attribute `a_param` missing in object `AB`

**Screenshots or Code**
```python
class AMixin:
    a_param: str

    def a(self):
        return self.a_param


class AB(AMixin):
    def __init__(self, b: str) -> None:
        self.b_param = b

    def b(self):
        return self.b_param


ab = AB("b")
ab.a() # will throw because `AB` object has no attribute `a_param`
```

**VS Code extension or command-line**
vscode pylance v2020.9.8
